requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which is not the case for indexed public datasets.

Defender pivot anticipated. A dome advocate may concede the post-hoc framing and pivot to: 'the 2.26× ratio IS verifiable — a velocity-component reconstruction over a 1990–2025 km/yr window recovers ≈2.16×, within 5% — and pointing at a real measured asymmetry that standard physics also doesn’t independently derive is a kind of explanatory work, even if not a priori prediction.' Three counters preempt this. (1) Verifiability is not derivation. Reproducing a number from a public dataset (NOAA NP.xy spans 1590–2025 and predates the dome model by decades) is the operational definition of post-hoc retrodiction; it cannot be cited as a defense against the post-hoc objection because it is the post-hoc objection. The dome did not predict 2.26 from its parameters before measurement — it adopted the value after the measurement existed. (2) Pointing at the asymmetry is not explanatory if the dome points at it inconsistently. The dome’s own weekly test W019 (on its predictions page) registered a directional claim about the same NMP asymmetry (“poleward dominates lateral”) and was REFINED by the author with notes that observed lateral drift is 5.5× larger than poleward — a value 2.4× higher than WIN-043’s claimed 2.26× (see additional detail below). A model that produces two different numbers for the same physical asymmetry, both labeled as confirmation, is not pointing — it is reading off whatever the data says. (3) The geometry could not have produced this number even in principle. The dome’s H(r) field equation depends only on radial distance from the disc center (see The Axial Symmetry Paradox); it cannot generate longitude-dependent quantities of any magnitude. The 2.26× ratio is structurally inaccessible to dome geometry, regardless of how exactly the data reproduces it.
patches.rationale: WIN-043.c3 advocate_mode (defense_survives=3) preemptive_recommendation (a): "acknowledge the value is reproducible from public data, but reproducibility-from-public-data is the definition of post-hoc retrodiction, not a defense against it." Plus advocate_mode preemptive (b): "add the W019 self-contradiction." Plus EXP-297 tracker note: "verifiable from NOAA data ≠ derived from dome geometry a priori; dome did not predict 2.26 from its geometry before measurement. Plus W019 FALSIFIED means the dome-predicted directional dominance is falsified, undercutting the mechanism claim." The labeled "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph follows the EXP-295 (WIN-063 P3) and EXP-296 (WIN-066 P1) convention. Three counters: (1) verifiability ≠ derivation — reproducing a number from public data IS the post-hoc objection, not a defense; (2) inconsistent pointing (2.26× vs W019’s 5.5× for the same asymmetry) defeats the "pointing is explanatory" rhetorical move; (3) structural impossibility from axial-symmetric H(r) (cross-ref Section 4). PRED-R004 forward-falsification is mentioned in the new detail_extra rather than detail_verdict_text to keep the verdict paragraph focused on the rebuttal itself.

patches.target_path: wins[id=043].detail_extra — set field from null to a new HTML string carrying (a) W019 self-contradiction, (b) Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference, (c) PRED-R004 forward-falsification flag.

changes.new:
The W019 self-contradiction. The dome’s own weekly test W019 was registered claiming poleward NMP drift would dominate over lateral. After observation, the author marked W019 REFINED on the basis that observed lateral drift is 5.5× larger than poleward — a value 2.4× higher than WIN-043’s claimed 2.26× ratio. The dome’s own monitoring suite therefore reports two different ratios for the same directional asymmetry (2.26× as a CONFIRMED past claim in WIN-043, 5.5× in W019’s current REFINED notes), without methodological reconciliation. The directional asymmetry is real — but the dome cannot produce a stable predicted value for it.

Structural impossibility. See The Axial Symmetry Paradox: the dome’s H(r) field equation depends only on radial distance, so by construction it cannot generate longitude-dependent quantities of any magnitude — 2.26× or otherwise. WIN-043 is one of multiple longitude-dependent claims cataloged there as structurally inaccessible to the dome’s radially symmetric geometry.

Forward falsification. The dome’s sibling claim PRED-R004 (NMP drift ratio ≥2.0× through 2028) recycles WIN-043 as a forward commitment. Quantitative checks of NOAA NP.xy show the 2024–2025 single-year km/yr ratio is 1.56× and decelerating per the post-2019 NMP slowdown (Chulliat et al. 2020). If the trend continues, PRED-R004 falsifies on its own terms within the test window — a recycled post-hoc retrodiction can become a failed prospective prediction.

The Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference reinforces the systemic structural argument cataloged in Section 4 (which explicitly names WIN-043 as a longitude-dependent claim from a radially symmetric model).

The dome’s own monitoring contradicts its CONFIRMED claim, but our text does not surface this.

P1 also references the W019 5.5× number inline in the defender-pivot rebuttal, so the contradiction is visible both in the verdict paragraph and the supporting detail.

holes_addressed.hole_summary: Dome defender argues the 2.26× ratio IS verifiable (we showed 2.16× ourselves) and pivots to "pointing at it is explanatory work." Calling it post-hoc treats every measurement as illegitimate (too strict).
holes_addressed.how_addressed: P1 "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph addresses all three prongs: (1) verifiability ≠ derivation — reproducibility-from-public-data IS the post-hoc objection, not a defense; (2) inconsistent pointing (2.26× vs 5.5×) defeats "pointing is explanatory"; (3) structural impossibility from axial-symmetric H(r). The post-hoc objection is not "too strict" — it is specifically about indexed public datasets per Part 3.1b, which NOAA NP.xy unambiguously is.

holes_not_addressed.hole_summary: Subtle wording: "explains" vs "predicts" for standard geophysics on the 2.26× ratio.
holes_not_addressed.reason: Curmudgeon explicitly noted "No change needed. Current "explains" framing is precise." No patch needed.

new_evidence_added: Inline reference to PRED-R004 forward falsification: 2024–2025 single-year km/yr ratio is 1.56× and decelerating per Chulliat et al. 2020. If trend continues, the recycled claim falsifies within the test window. Sources the forward-test evidence the C3 review computed but did not yet integrate into WIN-043 text.
Quantification of W019 5.5× vs WIN-043 2.26× as a 2.4× internal discrepancy. The previous text mentioned neither number in this contrastive form.
Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference using verified anchor #axial-symmetry (corrects the curmudgeon’s suggested #axial-symmetry-paradox which does not match the current HTML id).

anticipated_objections.objection: "You’re assigning weight to W019’s 5.5× number even though the dome marked W019 REFINED, not CONFIRMED — a refined status is a self-correction, not a contradiction."
anticipated_objections.response: The dome’s status taxonomy itself does not rescue this. WIN-043 is CONFIRMED (with the dome’s strongest endorsement) at 2.26×. W019 is REFINED (a softer status) but with author-supplied notes that explicitly state lateral dominates poleward by 5.5×. Both are statements about the same physical asymmetry made in the dome’s own published artifacts. A self-correcting model that revises a directional CLAIM is fine; a model that holds two different numerical RATIOS for the same phenomenon — one as CONFIRMED, the other as REFINED — without methodological reconciliation has not self-corrected, it has self-contradicted. The dome’s revision narrative would require dropping or updating WIN-043’s 2.26× in light of the W019 5.5× finding; instead WIN-043 remains CONFIRMED.

anticipated_objections.objection: "PRED-R004 talks about ratio ≥2.0× over a multi-year window, not the 2024–2025 single-year value. You’re cherry-picking the most adverse window for the dome."
anticipated_objections.response: Granted that PRED-R004 commits to a multi-year average through 2028, not a single-year minimum. Our P2 forward-falsification language is appropriately hedged: it says "if the trend continues" — not "PRED-R004 has falsified." The 1.56× single-year value is presented as a deceleration data point worth tracking, not a settled falsification. The C3 quantitative_verification field documents that the multi-window average over 1990–2025 currently stands at 2.16×, which is still above the 2.0 threshold. The forward-falsification flag is genuinely forward-looking; it asks the reader to watch the trend, not to count it as already failed.

anticipated_objections.objection: "The Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference is circular — you’re using your own structural argument as evidence against the dome’s claim."
anticipated_objections.response: The Axial Symmetry Paradox section is itself derived from the dome’s published H(r) equation and the dome’s own claim that the geometry is radially symmetric. It does not import an external assumption — it shows that a radially symmetric scalar field cannot, as a mathematical fact, produce longitude-dependent outputs. This is closer to dimensional analysis than to argument: a function f(r) of radius alone has no φ dependence by construction. The cross-reference adds a structural impossibility layer to the post-hoc and self-contradiction layers — three independent failure modes, each derived from the dome’s own published material.

kernel_of_truth.statement: The directional asymmetry of NMP drift is real and quantitatively significant: longitudinal velocity has dominated latitudinal velocity over the post-1990 trajectory. Independent quantitative reconstruction recovers ~2.16× over 1990–2025 in km/yr units (within 5% of the dome’s claimed 2.26×). The dome correctly identifies a feature most lay readers would not notice without prompting, and its weekly test W019 was registered specifically to track this asymmetry. There IS a real underlying signal here.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three independent failure layers persist after granting the kernel. (1) Post-hoc retrodiction: NOAA NP.xy is publicly indexed and predates the dome model by decades; reproducing a value from indexed public data is the operational definition of post-hoc retrodiction, which Part 3.1b specifically targets. (2) Self-contradiction: the dome’s own W019 reports 5.5× lateral:poleward (REFINED), 2.4× higher than WIN-043’s 2.26× CONFIRMED — the dome cannot produce a stable predicted value. (3) Structural impossibility: the dome’s H(r) field equation is axially symmetric and cannot generate longitude-dependent quantities of any magnitude. These three failures are independent: each survives the dome’s strongest defense on the others, and any one of them would be sufficient to disqualify WIN-043 as a derivation rather than a description.

test_recommendation: tests/run.sh after build.js html should pass. The patches are prose-only (no DATA blob, no numeric counts, no rate fields, no classification fields) so existing numeric-claim tests are not affected. Recommend a future test addition: a unit test that detail_verdict_text contains the string "Defender pivot anticipated" for WIN-043 — mirrors the EXP-296 test recommendation and guards against silent regressions if the prose is rewritten and the defender-pivot rebuttal is dropped. Also recommend a test that detail_extra is non-null for WIN-043 (currently null prior to this patch).

closure_recommendation_for_decider.closure_note: EXP-297 P1 (find/replace adding "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph to WIN-043.detail_verdict_text) preempts all three prongs of the defender pivot: (a) verifiability ≠ derivation; (b) W019 self-contradiction; (c) structural impossibility from axial-symmetric H(r). EXP-297 P2 (field_set on detail_extra from null to new HTML) carries the W019 self-contradiction in detail with the 2.26× vs 5.5× contrast, the Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference, and the PRED-R004 forward-falsification flag. After patch integration, the C3 hole #1 (major) and hole #2 (moderate, carryover) are fully closed, and the ISS-1918 defender-pivot pattern is structurally rebutted in the page text. ISS-1918 should close to fixed.

scope_note: Two-patch defense expansion (matches EXP-296 single-patch + a new field_set for the previously-null detail_extra). Justified because the WIN-043.c3 review identified two distinct content gaps: (a) advocate_mode preemption in detail_verdict_text (P1) and (b) factual content additions to detail_extra (P2 — W019 self-contradiction and Axial Symmetry cross-reference). Combining them into a single patch would have produced a less-auditable diff; splitting them into two patches keeps the rhetorical layer (P1) separate from the factual-content layer (P2).
tldr: ISS-1918 / WIN-043.c3 (defense_survives=3) defender pivot — "the 2.26× ratio IS verifiable from NOAA data; standard physics doesn’t derive it either; pointing is explanatory work" — preempted by two-patch sequence. P1 adds labeled "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph to detail_verdict_text with three counters (verifiability ≠ derivation; W019 5.5× vs 2.26× self-contradiction; axial-symmetric H(r) cannot produce longitude-dependent quantities). P2 sets previously-null detail_extra to new HTML with W019 self-contradiction, Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference, and PRED-R004 forward-falsification flag.
